title: China wants Trump to embrace its vision of peace for Taiwan
subhead: Beijing has made the island its top priority, hoping to convince US President Donald Trump, who is visiting China from May 13 to 15, to shift his stance on Taiwan's independence and hold back on selling arms to the island nation.

United Kingdom local elections: 'The entire party system has yet to recover from the fractures caused by Brexit'
subhead: After the Labour Party's failure in the local elections on May 7, it is time for the party to consider policies addressing territorial, economic and social inequalities, argues political scientist Florence Faucher in an op-ed for Le Monde.

title: Obesity surges in low-income countries, stabilizes in wealthier nations
subhead: A major study on global obesity trends, published Wednesday in Nature, reveals diverging trajectories depending on a country's income level. Obesity, however, has reached alarming heights worldwide, affecting one in eight people.

title: PSG clinch fifth straight Ligue 1 title
subhead: The reigning European champions will play Arsenal in this season's Champions League final on May 30.

title: Massoumeh Lahidji, the polyglot watching over the Cannes Film Festival
subhead: The Iranian interpreter, a pillar of the festival, translates without notes and collaborates with numerous filmmakers.

'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
subhead: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: How one family and their shipping company dominate Marseille
subhead: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (3/4). Based in France's second-largest city, the company led by Rodolphe Saadé plays a major role in the city but has become less visible since the controversy over excess profits.
